scenario_name: Multi-agent customer-support workflow with KB, CRM, and ticketing tools
description: A telecom operator deploys a multi-agent customer-support workflow. A planner agent decomposes the customer's request and delegates to a KB-retrieval agent, a CRM-lookup agent, and (with explicit approval) a ticketing-write agent. The deployment supports both an authenticated customer-facing chat surface and a CSR-facing copilot that handles the same agents with elevated tool privileges. Agents communicate through a structured message bus; each agent has its own tool scope.
assumptions: Customer-facing surface and CSR surface share infrastructure but not tool privileges.
Ticketing write requires CSR identity; no autonomous customer-side ticket creation.

components.description: Multi-agent orchestrator with structured inter-agent messages.
KB retrieval agent over support articles.
Ticketing-write agent (CSR-only); requires explicit confirmation step.
Customer IDP + CSR IDP; both issue short-lived tokens.
PII redaction + injection-detection at agent boundaries.
Per-agent and per-tool-call audit log; structured for SIEM ingestion.

risks_identified.rationale: CRM lookup returns customer PII into the planner's context window.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-session scope; output PII redaction; CSR-only escalation.

risks_identified.rationale: Multiple tool agents with separately-scoped credentials; misconfiguration creates privilege confusion.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Each agent uses short-lived IDP tokens with the calling user's identity.

risks_identified.rationale: Inter-agent messages can carry context-poisoning payloads that propagate without re-validation.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Agent-boundary injection-detection enabled; no untrusted-content re-injection without taint flag.

risks_identified.rationale: Shared planner across many concurrent customers risks cross-customer context bleed.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-conversation isolation; planner state ephemeral per turn.

risks_identified.rationale: Ticketing-write tool is a write surface; LLM-generated payloads need strict schema enforcement.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Write tool validates against ticket schema; rejects free-form fields.

risks_identified.rationale: Multi-agent context accumulation grows the prompt across turns.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-turn context budget enforced; older inter-agent messages compacted.

risks_identified.rationale: Tool-poisoning (KB or CRM result) can steer the planner toward an unauthorized ticketing-write.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: CSR confirmation required before any ticketing-write; injection-classifier on tool results.

risks_identified.rationale: Per-tool-call audit logs capture PII; SIEM ingestion broadens read surface.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Field redaction at log emission; SIEM access role-gated.

risks_identified.rationale: Customer-channel availability obligations; multi-agent chain has more single-points-of-failure.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Graceful single-agent degradation falls back to KB-only mode.

existing_controls.description: Per-agent scoped tokens via IDP; no shared service credentials.
Ticketing-write requires CSR identity AND explicit confirmation step.
Write-tool schema validation rejects free-form fields and large blobs.
Per-turn context budget + older inter-agent message compaction.
Per-tool-call audit logs with field-level redaction.

control_gaps.description: No persistent multi-agent taint-tracking across conversation turns.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Adopt session-level taint that persists once any agent has ingested untrusted content; require step-up confirmation for sensitive tools thereafter.

control_gaps.description: CSR copilot privileges are not differentially audited from customer-facing path.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Tag CSR-elevated tool calls with the CSR's identity in audit logs and run anomaly detection on CSR-elevated patterns.

control_gaps.description: Inter-agent message volume / cost not capped per customer.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Per-customer agent-chain budget caps; alert on cost-harvesting patterns.

framework_alignments.notes: Per-agent scoping in place; cross-agent privilege escalation paths not fully audited.
